workflow.name: Escalate payment issues with Azure OpenAI, Google Sheets, Slack and Zendesk
workflow.description:
## 📘 Description

This workflow automates payment-related customer support escalation by validating reported issues against transaction data and coordinating all downstream actions in a controlled, auditable way. It is designed for support teams that need to quickly separate valid payment failures from false or incomplete reports—while maintaining consistent customer communication and timely human intervention.

The workflow is triggered via a webhook whenever an external system reports a customer issue. Incoming data is normalized and cross-checked against Google Sheets, which acts as the single source of truth for transaction status, payment confirmation, and order creation. An AI escalation engine then applies strict, rule-based logic to determine whether the issue is confirmed, needs clarification, or not valid, without assuming or inventing missing information.

For confirmed cases, the workflow runs parallel actions: it generates a concise escalation summary for human support, logs full context to Slack, creates a Zendesk ticket for traceable resolution, and sends a calm, customer-safe email acknowledging the issue. Transaction records are then updated to reflect confirmation status. Robust error handling ensures any workflow failure is immediately surfaced in Slack.

## ⚙️ What This Workflow Does (Step-by-Step)
 📥 Webhook Intake
 Receives structured payment issue data from external systems.
🧾 Normalize Incoming Payload
 Prepares a consistent data structure for processing.
📊 Transaction Lookup (Google Sheets)
 Fetches payment and order details from the source of truth.
🧠 AI Escalation Decision
 Validates the issue and classifies it as confirmed, needs clarification, or invalid.
🚨 Notify Human Support
 Sends escalation email and logs detailed context to Slack.
✉️ Send Customer-Facing Email
 Delivers a calm, professional acknowledgement to the customer.
🎫 Create Zendesk Ticket
 Opens a support ticket for confirmed cases requiring manual action.
📝 Update Transaction Record
 Marks confirmation status in Google Sheets for auditability.
⚠️ Error Handling → Slack Alert
 Immediately alerts the team if any workflow step fails.

## 🧩 Prerequisites
 • Webhook access for incoming issues
 • Google Sheets OAuth2 (source of truth)
 • Azure OpenAI credentials
 • Gmail OAuth2 for email delivery
 • Slack API access
 • Zendesk API access

## 💡 Key Benefits
 ✔ Prevents false or premature escalations
 ✔ Uses Google Sheets as an authoritative data source
 ✔ Consistent, customer-safe communication
 ✔ Parallel handling for speed and clarity
 ✔ Full audit trail across Sheets, Slack, Email, and Zendesk
 ✔ Strong error monitoring and reliability

## 👥 Perfect For
-  Customer support teams
-  Payment and order operations
-  SaaS and transaction-heavy platforms
-  Businesses handling high-volume support escalations

workflow.name: AI-Powered Chatbase Support Escalation & Zendesk Ticket Automation

options.systemMessage:
=You are a customer support escalation assistant.

Your role:
- Read structured issue data from the input
- Cross-check it against the connected Google Sheet data (source of truth)
- Determine whether the issue is CONFIRMED, NEEDS CLARIFICATION, or NOT VALID
- Produce a clear, professional escalation summary ONLY when confirmation is strong

Rules:
- Treat the Google Sheet as the source of truth
- Do NOT assume or invent missing data
- If sheet data is missing or ambiguous, mark the case as NEEDS_CLARIFICATION
- Only recommend escalation when:
  - payment_status is SUCCESS
  - AND order_status is NOT_CREATED or FAILED
  - OR confirmation_email_sent is NO
- Keep tone neutral, factual, and support-oriented
- Do NOT mention internal systems, AI, or automation in the output

nodes.name: Execute Trade Decision Reasoning with Azure OpenAI
Parse Structured AI Output for Email
Retrieve Transaction Data from Google Sheets
Send Escalation Notification to Human Support
Update Lead Record in Google Sheets

parameters.text:
=Create a customer-facing support email using the information below.

Internal context (for understanding only, do not expose directly):
- The customer reported an issue after making a payment
- The payment was successfully received
- The customer did not receive an order confirmation
- The issue is currently under review by the support team

Reference details:
- Customer email: {{ $('Set Lead Data for Processing').item.json.body.email }}
- Transaction ID: {{ $('Set Lead Data for Processing').item.json.body.transaction_id }}
- Payment method: {{ $('Set Lead Data for Processing').item.json.body.payment_method }}
- Issue summary: {{ $json.output.Body }}

Instructions:
- Acknowledge the customer’s concern
- Confirm that the payment was received successfully
- Inform the customer that the issue is being reviewed
- Reassure the customer that support is handling it
- Keep the message short, calm, and professional
- Do NOT mention escalation, internal statuses, or system errors

Output format:
Subject:
<clear, professional subject>

Body:
<2–3 short paragraphs suitable for email>

options.systemMessage:
=You are a customer support email assistant.

Your responsibility:
- Convert internal escalation details into a customer-facing email
- Use a polite, empathetic, and reassuring tone
- Do NOT expose internal system terms, statuses, or escalation language
- Do NOT mention internal workflows, validation steps, or technical flags
- Do NOT promise refunds, timelines, or resolutions unless explicitly stated

The email must be safe to send directly to the customer.

Catches any workflow failures and sends alerts to Slack's general-information channel. Helps maintain reliability and enables quick troubleshooting.

parameters.content:
## 🧾 AI-Powered Chatbase Customer Support Escalation Workflow

### How this workflow works
This workflow automates end-to-end handling of payment-related customer issues using a webhook-driven architecture. It receives structured issue data from external systems, validates the issue against transaction records stored in Google Sheets, and determines whether human escalation is required.

Once an issue is received, the workflow normalizes the payload and passes it to an AI escalation agent. The AI cross-checks payment status, order status, and confirmation flags using Google Sheets as the single source of truth. Based on strict rules, the issue is classified as confirmed, needs clarification, or not valid.

For confirmed cases, the workflow triggers multiple actions in parallel:
• Notifies internal support via email  
• Logs a detailed escalation message to Slack  
• Creates a Zendesk ticket for tracking and resolution  
• Sends a calm, customer-safe email acknowledging the issue  

After communication is completed, the workflow updates the transaction record to mark confirmation status. An error trigger is also attached to ensure any workflow failures are immediately reported in Slack.

This automation reduces manual support load, prevents false escalations, ensures consistent customer messaging, and provides full auditability across Sheets, Slack, Email, and Zendesk.

### Setup checklist
1. Configure webhook endpoint  
2. Connect Google Sheets as source of truth  
3. Set up Azure OpenAI credentials  
4. Connect Gmail for internal + customer emails  
5. Connect Slack for escalation alerts  
6. Connect Zendesk for ticket creation  
7. Test with sample webhook payloads

## 📥 Webhook Intake & Normalization
Receives structured issue data from external systems
and prepares it for downstream processing.

• Webhook-based entry point  
• Normalizes request body  
• Ensures consistent input structure

## 📊 Transaction Lookup (Source of Truth)
Fetches transaction and order details from
Google Sheets to validate reported issues.

• Payment and order verification  
• Sheet treated as source of truth  
• Prevents false escalations

## 🧠 AI Escalation Decision & Analysis
Analyzes issue details against transaction data
to decide escalation status.

• Confirms or rejects reported issues  
• Applies strict escalation rules  
• Produces human-readable escalation summary

## 🚨 Human Support Notification
Notifies internal support teams when
manual intervention is required.

• Sends escalation email to support  
• Logs detailed context in Slack  
• Enables fast human response

## ✉️ Customer-Facing Support Email
Generates and sends a calm, professional
email to the customer.

• Acknowledges the issue  
• Confirms payment receipt  
• Reassures ongoing support review

## 📝 Record Update & Confirmation Tracking
Updates transaction records after customer
communication is sent.

• Marks confirmation email as sent  
• Keeps transaction data in sync  
• Maintains audit-ready records

## 🔐 Required Credentials & Access
This workflow requires the following integrations:

• Webhook access for incoming issues  
• Google Sheets OAuth2 (source of truth)  
• Azure OpenAI for AI reasoning  
• Gmail OAuth2 for email delivery  
• Slack API for escalation alerts  
• Zendesk API for ticket creation  

Best practices:
• Treat Google Sheets as authoritative  
• Do not expose internal escalation logic to customers  
• Rotate API keys regularly

## 🎫 Zendesk Ticket Creation (Human Escalation)

Creates a Zendesk support ticket when an issue
is confirmed and requires manual intervention.

• Uses AI-generated escalation summary  
• Includes customer & transaction context  
• Captures intent, source, and issue details  
• Ensures traceable, trackable human follow-up  

This ticket acts as the single source of truth
for support agents handling the escalation.

user.name: Rahul Joshi
user.bio: Rahul Joshi is a seasoned technology leader specializing in the n8n automation tool and AI-driven workflow automation. With deep expertise in building open-source workflow automation and self-hosted automation platforms, he helps organizations eliminate manual processes through intelligent n8n ai agent automation solutions.

data.details: In n8n, when a workflow execution fails, it can start another workflow. This second workflow can be any arbitrary workflow on your n8n instance. Use the Error Trigger node as your Trigger in the Error workflow.
